scene.description: Engine 181 is pumping a standpipe operation at a 5-story apartment building. Crews are operating from the 5th-floor standpipe outlet, which is 40 feet above pump level using the floor-height rule (1st floor = 0 feet, each floor above adds 10 feet). The crew has 150’ of 2½” hose and a 1⅛” smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI. Add 25 PSI for standpipe/FDC system loss. What pump pressure should the driver/operator set?

nozzles: 1⅛” smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI
sceneElements.elevation: 5th floor outlet is 40 feet above pump level, +20 PSI

problems.title: Advanced Standpipe Floor 5
problems.question: Engine 181 is supplying a 5th-floor standpipe operation through 100 ft of 2½-inch hose to the FDC. Crews have 150 ft of 2½-inch hose from the 5th-floor standpipe outlet to a 1⅛-inch smooth bore nozzle flowing 265 GPM at 50 PSI. The 5th-floor outlet is 40 ft above pump level, so add 20 PSI elevation. Add 25 PSI for standpipe/FDC system loss. What pump discharge pressure should the driver/operator set?
formulaBreakdown: PP = FDC supply line FL + interior attack line FL + nozzle pressure + elevation + standpipe/FDC loss.
problems.instructorExplanation: A complete standpipe/FDC pump-pressure path includes the hose from Engine 181 to the FDC, the interior attack hose from the standpipe outlet to the nozzle, nozzle pressure, elevation, and the standpipe/FDC system loss.
problems.explainMistake: Do not calculate only the interior handline when the engine is pumping through an FDC supply hose. Include both friction-loss sections before adding nozzle pressure, elevation, and system loss.
